the Fair Politicat Practices

g
From time to time, one or another of my various clients, or
on some occasions several clients, ask me to make a contribution
to a political 
candidate or to support a proposition or other
batlot measure with a contribution. 
On most of those occasions,
because of client relationships, I do so. In addition, from time
to time, I reguest clients to make similar contributions in
response to reguests made to me by a political 
candidate or one
of his supporters or even by another client. 
In aII of these
instances the actual source of the cash is clearly shown by the
name on the check. In each instance the contribution is
appropriately reported by the donor and, I assume, bY the donee.
However, after reading the statutes and interpretive regulations
I still 
have the following questions:
1. When I reguest a contribution from one or more of ny
clients and I receive a check from then evidencing their
contribution, if r transmit the check to the candidate or the
candidatets supporters, either at a dinner or by mai1, am I an
"intermediarytr under the terms of the reporting statutes and if
so what do I do to comply with the regulations to appropriately
make a report as an rrintermediarytt?
2. When a client solicits support from me and f make a
personal contribution evidenced by a check on which my name
appears and which is appropriatery reported in ny n.*-", is the
person reguesting the contribution an rintermediiryrr or does a
person become an ttintermediaryt! if I give the check to someone to
deliver to the candidate or to the candidaters representative and
I attend the political 
function or dinner for whilh the reguest


IVERSON, YOAKUM, PAPIANO E HATCH
Fair PoliticaL Practices commission
June 4, 1990
Page 2
is rnade? Is the answer different if a friend or someone in rny
fj.rm uses the tickets for which I have paid?
It is rny interpretation that the answer to all of these
questions is, trnotr and that so long as the name of the person
rnaking the contribution appears on the check and that person
reports the contribution as required along srith, of course, the
appropriate report of the candidate, no further reporting is
necessary. I should appreciate it, however, if you will confirn
my understandinq in order that there can be no mistakes or future
difficulties.
Thank you very much for your consideration of this natter
and for your response.
HATCH
